metadata.title:
MAYOR MOORE WELCOMES
III DELEGATES, AND WITH MEN.
1TloN OF CLEANER SPOKANE,


AND CONVENTION GOES TO


WORK.

pages.raw_text:
I'VE had cleaning day and bon
hres In this city some days ago,
and since election we have had an
other cleaning, but not the bonfires
yet" stated Mayor Moore in an
address OF welcome this morning to
the Washington State Federation of
Women's Clubs, now In annual con-
vention at Masonic temple. The
mayors address was short and to
the point. ell thanked the women
generally for the gerat assistance
they lent to his election. He was
great favorite with the ladies this
morning. -


The meeting was called to order
at $3.50 o'clock, following the regis
tering of more than $50 women,
delegates and visitors. Just at
noon trainload more arrived from
Seattle and Tacoma, swelling the
attendance to more than 800. Fol-
lowing the hymn, ..'Blest Be the Tie
That Binds" Res. w. ]. Hindley
delivered the inHpcation.


Mrs. Sarah aPlatt Decker, the
national president, delivered an elo-
quent address on the work of the
clubs. Mrs. M. A. Phelps spoke on
Daughters Of the American Revo-
luton" Mrs. s. X. Green followed
with Daughters of Revolution" :
A piano solo by Miss Jessie Pat
ton OF the Ladies' Musical club of
North Yakima was the next num-
Der.


Following Mayor Moore's short
talk, Mrs. H. w. Allen, who seems
to be a strong choice as the next
president of the organization, de.
livered the address of welcome on
behalf of the Spokane women. In
response Mrs. w. p. Harper of Sc
attic spoke.


A double quartet. "The Spring"
furnished some excellent music. fol-
lowing roll call in the afternoon.
The rest of the session was given
over to reports Of committees and
discussion.


The president of the state feder-
alton, Miss S. M. Lombard, will
deliver her annual address this eve
ming. Mrs. Evelyn Chantler will
render her report as general feder-
alton secretary.


This evening the Wagner club
will give recital at the high school
for the benefit of the clubs.
